授業目的: To review the history of religions and religiousity in Japan from pre-history till now.To gain familiarity with religious organizations and their teachings through studies of primary and secondary texts within the framework of the course book.To polish the academic skills in the field of the study of religion.
到達目標: The participant will gain an introductory level of knowledge about the history, teachings, and characteristics of the main religions in Japan, Shinto, Buddhism, and Christianity.They will demonstratetheir grasp of them in a series of quizzes based on the textbook, present first-hand experiences from field studies, and demonstrate their academic skills in oral and written works.
授業方法: Face to face format. The classes are a combine of lectures, presentations, and discussions in groups and plenum.  Students are expected to have read the assigned materials prior to the class and be prepared to participate actively.

第1回: 【Startup Week】  "Online class（On-demand format）"／ 《90minutes》Introduction to the course. Answer the questions in the video (on LUNA), and upload a short video self-introduction as instructed.

Familiarize yourself with the course. Buy a copy of the textbook. Read Chapter 1 Encountering the Japanese Religous World: Introduction and Chapter 2 "The Past in the Present: Vignettes of Japanese Spiritual Life."
第2回:
【Core Weeks①～⑫】 "Face-to-face class"《90minutes》
Lecture and discussion: A brief outline of religiousity and the role of religons in Japan. Weather permitting, we will visit Uegahara Hachiman Shrine.

Take the quiz, and post a short essay = 1-2 pages A4 about your observations at the shrine. For next week, read Chapter 3 "The Way of the Kami: Shinto Then and Now."  Also read the description of Himiko in the history of Wei and Nihon Shoki. Texts mentioned here are availble on LUNA in the folder "Course Materials".
第3回:
【Core Weeks①～⑫】 "Face-to-face class"《90minutes》 The way of the Kami. 
Orientation about the field trip.

Take the quiz. For next week, Read "Interlude: Introduction to Buddhism" and chapter 4 "Early Times: Pre-Buddhist Japan and How Buddhism Arrived". Read also the source texts: excerpts from Nihon shoki and the Srimaladiva Simhanada Sutra.
第4回:
【Flex Hours】"Face-to-face class"《180 minutes》25 April SATURDAY. One-day (9-16) field study tour to places of worship in Kobe. 
Morning: Ikuta Jinja (Shinto shrine), Kobe Mosque, Kobe Central Catholic Church, Bhagwan Mahavirswami Jain Temple, Kitano Tenmangu (Shinto shrine). 
Lunch break.
Afternoon: Eiko Kyokai (United Church of Christ in Japan [protestant]), Kanteibyo (Chinese Temple), and the Nishi Honganji "Modern-dera" (Buddhist temple).

Follow the instructions given in class when you write a field study report, to be posted on LUNA. The report must include text, at least one photograph or sketch, and a poem. Cost: transportation fee and lunch. Solid and easy to slip on foot ware and an umbrella are recommended.
第5回: 【Core Weeks①～⑫】 "Face-to-face class"《90minutes》 Introduction to Buddhism and its arrival in Japan.
Take the quiz. For next week, Read chapter 5 "Magic Mountains and the Old Court: Heian Buddhism and its Culture." Recommended: chapters 2 and 25 of the Lotus Sutra.
第6回: 【Core Weeks①～⑫】 "Face-to-face class"《90minutes》 Buddhism - Nara and Heian age.

Take the quiz. For next week, Read chapter 6 "Warrior Culture, Simple Faith: The Kamakura Buddhist Reformation."
Group assignment: Visit one of the religious institutions on the list handed out in class, or one your group can agree on. Prepare a 10-minute presentation about the place and your visit there.
第7回: 【Core Weeks①～⑫】 "Face-to-face class"《90minutes》 Buddhism II - single practices
Take the quiz. For next week, Read chapter 7 "Swords and Satori: Zen and its Culture."
第8回: 【Core Weeks①～⑫】 "Face-to-face class"《90minutes》Buddhism "Swords and Satori: Zen and its Culture."
Take the quiz. For next week, get your presentation ready.
第9回: Write a review of your presentation and post it on LUNA. For next week, Read chapter 8 "Christ and Confucius: The West Arrives, and then Japan Turns Inward" and chapter 10 "Chanting and Dancing: Shugendo and the “New Religions” of Japan."
第10回: 【Core Weeks①～⑫】 "Face-to-face class"《90minutes》Movie-day. We will watch two documentaries. One about shugenja (mountain worshippers), the other about decendants of so-called hidden Christians. NB: This class will take place in the Library Hall Cinema.
第11回:
【Core Weeks①～⑫】 "Face-to-face class"《90minutes》 Chapter 8: Christ and Confucius
(Religions and other ideas in the Edo period (1600~1868))
Take the quiz. For next week, Read chapter 9 "The Rising Sun and the Dark Valley: From the Meiji Restoration until 1945"
第12回: 【Core Weeks①～⑫】 "Face-to-face class"《90minutes》 Religions in Japan 1868 to 1945
Take the quiz. For next week, read chapter 11 "Pilgrimages: Religion in Japan 1945 to Now."
第13回: 【Core Weeks①～⑫】 "Face-to-face class"《90minutes》Religions in Japan after 1945
Take the quiz. For next week, review the quizzes.
第14回:
【Core Weeks①～⑫】 "Face-to-face class"《90minutes》
 Exam in class with paper and pencil. It consists of questions selected from the quizzes and an analysis of a religious source.
Take the quiz. The exam consists of questions  selected from the quizzes and an analysis of a religious source - a shorter text or visual material.
トピック.授業外学習2: Extensive reading is encouraged. Instructions on reference/citation are given in class and must be followed. This includes identifying the software/App by name and version as well as the prompt every time you use GenAI. Disregarding this requirement will result in a low grade and possibly fail you.

教科書/Required texts0: The course is based on Robert Ellwood's "Japanese Religion: The 2nd Edition."   Also available at Amazon.co.jp.
参考書/Reference books2: Matthew D. McMullen and Jolyon Baraka Thomas eds.
New Nanzan Guide to Japanese Religions
参考文献・資料/Reference books0: The reference books are not mandatory but will most certainly prove helpful for your studies in this field.
学生による授業評価の方法Course Evaluation by Student:
授業中実施
Done online in class.

各授業科目は、各学部・研究科の定めるディプロマ・ポリシー（DP）・カリキュラム・ポリシー（CP）に基づき、カリキュラム上に配置されています。DP・CPを意識して本授業科目の学修を進めることで、各学部・研究科の期待する能力が養成されます。各学部・研究科のDP、CPや教育課程表（授業科目一覧等）は、下記リンクこちらに掲載していますので学修計画の参考にしてください。https://kwic.kwansei.ac.jp/cabinet/reference?typeCd=0&cabinetId=557&directLink=1※kwicへのリンクとなります（在学生のみ閲覧可）

成績評価Grading3: 授業への参加度（自発性、積極性、主体性、等）／In-class participation,contribution (09)
